steps.title: Attach the appositive finding
steps.content: The phrase after the blank, a finding that if true would overturn current theories, renames the preceding idea. It is an appositive noun phrase attached to the previous clause.

steps.title: Reject sentence breaks
steps.content: A has an incorrect space before the semicolon, B starts a sentence fragment, and C fuses the appositive to the clause without punctuation. A comma correctly attaches the appositive finding. The answer is D.
